title: Ilie Bărbulescu (footballer)

infobox.value: (24 June 1957 – 1 February 2020) was a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), and a member of [Steaua București](/wiki/CSA_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti_(football) "CSA Steaua București (football)")'s winning squad for the [European Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") in 1986 and for the [European Supercup](/wiki/European_Supercup "European Supercup") in 1987.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)

outgoing_links.target_title: 1986 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 3

**Ilie Bărbulescu** (24 June 1957 – 1 February 2020) was a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), and a member of [Steaua București](/wiki/CSA_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti_(football) "CSA Steaua București (football)")'s winning squad for the [European Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") in 1986 and for the [European Supercup](/wiki/European_Supercup "European Supercup") in 1987.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ilie_B%C4%83rbulescu_(footballer)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

Bărbulescu, nicknamed "Balamuc" (Madhouse), was born on 24 June 1957 in [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești"), Romania and began playing junior-level football in 1971 at local club [Argeș](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești") where he worked with coach Leonte Ianovschi.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2) He made his [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") debut on 9 November 1974 under coach [Florin Halagian](/wiki/Florin_Halagian "Florin Halagian") in a 1–1 draw against [FCM Reșița](/wiki/CSM_Re%C8%99i%C8%9Ba "CSM Reșița").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) In the [1978–79 UEFA Cup](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_UEFA_Cup "1978–79 UEFA Cup") he helped Argeș eliminate [Panathinaikos](/wiki/Panathinaikos_F.C. "Panathinaikos F.C.") in the first round with a 5–1 aggregate victory.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[4]](#cite_note-WF-4) In the following one they met [Valencia](/wiki/Valencia_CF "Valencia CF") led by [Mario Kempes](/wiki/Mario_Kempes "Mario Kempes"), earning a 2–1 win in the first leg, but they lost the second one with 5–2, thus the campaign ended.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[4]](#cite_note-WF-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) In the [same season](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_Divizia_A "1978–79 Divizia A") he helped Argeș win the title, being used by coach Halagian in 30 games in which he scored one goal.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[6]](#cite_note-RSC-6) In the following season they defeated [AEK Athens](/wiki/AEK_Athens_F.C. "AEK Athens F.C.") in the first round of the [1979–80 European Cup](/wiki/1979%E2%80%9380_European_Cup "1979–80 European Cup"), the team being eliminated in the following one by title holders and eventual winners, [Nottingham Forest](/wiki/Nottingham_Forest_F.C. "Nottingham Forest F.C.") against whom Bărbulescu scored once.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 1982 he joined [Olt Scornicești](/wiki/FC_Olt_Scornice%C8%99ti "FC Olt Scornicești") where he worked once again with Halagian.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)[[8]](#cite_note-wesport-8) After one and a half years he went to play for [Petrolul Ploiești](/wiki/FC_Petrolul_Ploie%C8%99ti "FC Petrolul Ploiești").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)

In 1984, Bărbulescu joined [Steaua București](/wiki/CSA_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti_(football) "CSA Steaua București (football)"), reuniting for a while with Halagian, helping the club win [The Double](/wiki/Double_(association_football) "Double (association football)") in his [first season](/wiki/1984%E2%80%9385_Divizia_A "1984–85 Divizia A") as Halagian and [Emerich Jenei](/wiki/Emerich_Jenei "Emerich Jenei") used him in 30 league matches but did not play in the 2–1 victory in the [Cupa României final](/wiki/1985_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei_final "1985 Cupa României final") over [Universitatea Craiova](/wiki/CS_Universitatea_Craiova "CS Universitatea Craiova").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)[[6]](#cite_note-RSC-6)[[8]](#cite_note-wesport-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) In the [following season](/wiki/1985%E2%80%9386_Divizia_A "1985–86 Divizia A"), he won the league title, scoring two goals in the 33 appearances given to him by Jenei.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)[[6]](#cite_note-RSC-6)[[10]](#cite_note-presamil-10) The coach also used him in all nine games in the historical [European Cup](/wiki/1985%E2%80%9386_European_Cup "1985–86 European Cup") campaign, in which Bărbulescu scored a goal from 25 meters against [Budapest Honvéd](/wiki/Budapest_Honv%C3%A9d_FC "Budapest Honvéd FC").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)[[10]](#cite_note-presamil-10)[[11]](#cite_note-tiki-11) He then played the entire match, including [extra time](/wiki/Overtime_(sports) "Overtime (sports)"), in the eventual 2–0 victory after the penalty shoot-out in the [final](/wiki/1986_European_Cup_final "1986 European Cup final") against [Barcelona](/wiki/FC_Barcelona "FC Barcelona").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)[[10]](#cite_note-presamil-10)[[11]](#cite_note-tiki-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) After goalkeeper [Helmut Duckadam](/wiki/Helmut_Duckadam "Helmut Duckadam") defended the last penalty, Bărbulescu immediately went and picked up the trophy and kissed it, receiving a [$](/wiki/United_States_dollar "United States dollar")20,000 fine from [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA").[[13]](#cite_note-fine-13) Eventually, Steaua's president, [Ion Alecsandrescu](/wiki/Ion_Alecsandrescu "Ion Alecsandrescu") spoke to [Valentin Ceaușescu](/wiki/Valentin_Ceau%C8%99escu "Valentin Ceaușescu") and [Yiorgos Vardinogiannis](/wiki/Yiorgos_Vardinogiannis "Yiorgos Vardinogiannis"), who helped him get rid of the fine.[[13]](#cite_note-fine-13) He started the [1986–87](/wiki/1986%E2%80%9387_Divizia_A "1986–87 Divizia A") season by playing all the minutes in the 1–0 win over [Dynamo Kyiv](/wiki/FC_Dynamo_Kyiv "FC Dynamo Kyiv") in the [European Super Cup](/wiki/1986_European_Super_Cup "1986 European Super Cup") and in the loss in the [Intercontinental Cup](/wiki/1986_Intercontinental_Cup "1986 Intercontinental Cup") with the same score against [River Plate](/wiki/Club_Atl%C3%A9tico_River_Plate "Club Atlético River Plate").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[14]](#cite_note-14) At the match against River Plate in [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"), representatives of the [Canon](/wiki/Canon_EOS "Canon EOS") company gave Bărbulescu their latest Canon camera, a gesture stemming from an incident during the 1986 European Cup final against Barcelona.[[10]](#cite_note-presamil-10) In that match, he famously crashed into a Canon-inscribed billboard after a shoulder-to-shoulder duel with [Lobo Carrasco](/wiki/Lobo_Carrasco "Lobo Carrasco"), an incident the company found particularly memorable.[[10]](#cite_note-presamil-10) He finished the season by winning another Double with Steaua, as coaches Jenei and [Anghel Iordănescu](/wiki/Anghel_Iord%C4%83nescu "Anghel Iordănescu") gave him 14 league appearances in which he scored once, also appearing for the full 90 minutes in the 1–0 victory in the [Cupa României final](/wiki/1987_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei_final "1987 Cupa României final") over [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[6]](#cite_note-RSC-6)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In 1987, Bărbulescu made a comeback to Petrolul Ploiești, but after half a year he went for a second spell at Argeș Pitești where on 19 March 1988 he made his last Divizia A appearance in a 1–0 home loss to Universitatea Craiova.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)[[16]](#cite_note-16) He accumulated a total of 357 matches and 22 goals scored in the Romanian top-league and 23 games with three goals in European competitions.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2) Bărbulescu ended his career in 1991, after playing in the Romanian lower leagues for [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești") and [Callatis Mangalia](/wiki/Callatis_Mangalia "Callatis Mangalia"), helping the latter promote from the [third league](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III") to the [second](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-GSP-2)[[17]](#cite_note-ziua-17)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ilie_B%C4%83rbulescu_(footballer)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International career")]

Bărbulescu played five games for [Romania](/wiki/Romania_national_football_team "Romania national football team"), making his debut on 13 May 1979 under coach [Florin Halagian](/wiki/Florin_Halagian "Florin Halagian") in a 1–1 draw against [Cyprus](/wiki/Cyprus_national_football_team "Cyprus national football team") in the [Euro 1980 qualifiers](/wiki/UEFA_Euro_1980_qualifying_Group_3 "UEFA Euro 1980 qualifying Group 3").[[18]](#cite_note-EU-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) He also played in a 3–1 win over [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey_national_football_team "Turkey national football team") in the [1986 World Cup qualifiers](/wiki/1986_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_UEFA_Group_3 "1986 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 3") which took place at the [İzmir Atatürk Stadium](/wiki/%C4%B0zmir_Atat%C3%BCrk_Stadium "İzmir Atatürk Stadium").[[18]](#cite_note-EU-18) Afterwards he made his last appearance for the national team on 4 March 1987 in a friendly against Turkey which ended with another 3–1 victory, played at the [Ankara 19 Mayıs Stadium](/wiki/Ankara_19_May%C4%B1s_Stadium "Ankara 19 Mayıs Stadium").[[18]](#cite_note-EU-18)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ilie_B%C4%83rbulescu_(footballer)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Bărbulescu was awarded the Honorary Citizen of [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești") title.[[20]](#cite_note-curier-20)[[21]](#cite_note-argesul-21)

On 25 March 2008 he was decorated by the president of [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), [Traian Băsescu](/wiki/Traian_B%C4%83sescu "Traian Băsescu") with Ordinul "Meritul Sportiv" — (The Order "The Sportive Merit") class II for his part in winning the [1985–86 European Cup](/wiki/1985%E2%80%9386_European_Cup "1985–86 European Cup").[[20]](#cite_note-curier-20)[[21]](#cite_note-argesul-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ilie_B%C4%83rbulescu_(footballer)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Death")]

Aged 62, Bărbulescu was found dead by his wife in their home in Pitești on 1 February 2020, after suffering a heart attack in his sleep.[[24]](#cite_note-24) Around 600 people participated at his funeral, held in his native Pitești.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ilie_B%C4%83rbulescu_(footballer)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Honours")]

**Argeș Pitești**

* [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"): [1978–79](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_Divizia_A "1978–79 Divizia A")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)

**Steaua București**

* [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"): [1984–85](/wiki/1984%E2%80%9385_Divizia_A "1984–85 Divizia A"), [1985–86](/wiki/1985%E2%80%9386_Divizia_A "1985–86 Divizia A"), [1986–87](/wiki/1986%E2%80%9387_Divizia_A "1986–87 Divizia A")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)
* [Cupa României](/wiki/Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Cupa României"): [1984–85](/wiki/1984%E2%80%9385_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1984–85 Cupa României"), [1986–87](/wiki/1986%E2%80%9387_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1986–87 Cupa României")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)
* [European Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League"): [1985–86](/wiki/1985%E2%80%9386_European_Cup "1985–86 European Cup")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)
* [European Supercup](/wiki/European_Supercup "European Supercup"): [1986](/wiki/1986_European_Super_Cup "1986 European Super Cup")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)
* [Intercontinental Cup](/wiki/Intercontinental_Cup_(1960%E2%80%932004) "Intercontinental Cup (1960–2004)") runner-up: [1986](/wiki/1986_Intercontinental_Cup "1986 Intercontinental Cup")[[26]](#cite_note-26)

**Callatis Mangalia**

* [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"): [1989–90](/wiki/1989%E2%80%9390_Divizia_C "1989–90 Divizia C")[[17]](#cite_note-ziua-17)
